reasoning: A is the clear winner: its five forms span the widest conceptual range (system-audit, husbandry manual, wordless emotional MRI, prediction market grading the adults, negative-space indictment of the instrument), and several ideas are genuinely non-obvious. The Bet Slip (graded predictors, "low expectations" as a losing public position) and the wordless False-Color Scan are the boldest and most generative ideas in the whole field, and the prose is the most concrete and alive ("a sensor reading on a malfunctioning building," "over-watered by praise"). B is a strong second with crisp borrowed frames (Friction Log, Portfolio of Wrongness, Seismograph reporting derivatives not levels) and excellent craft, but it leans on familiar metaphors and is a half-step less surprising. C and D cover heavily overlapping territory with A and B (Wake, environment-grading, artifact/object proof, longitudinal capsule); C edges D because the Confidence Map (scoring calibration, confidently-wrong as worst) and Open Bench jury add fresher angles, whereas D is the most conventional, padded with an explicit framework preamble and a leaked internal file path that adds nothing for the reader.
